title: How I use Claude Code: Separation of Planning and Execution
description: Boris Tane shares insights from 9 months of intensive Claude Code usage. Core principle: 'Never let Claude write a single line of code before I've reviewed and approved a written plan.' Workflow cycle (1-6 iterations): Research → Planning → Annotation Iteration → Todo List → Implementation → Feedback Iteration. Separating planning from execution significantly reduces waste, helps maintain control over architectural decisions, and achieves better results with minimal tokens. The most important thing for complex tasks is investing time in upfront planning.
author: Boris Tane

title: Llama 3.1 70B on a Single RTX 3090 via NVMe-to-GPU Bypass
description: NTransformer project achieves running Llama 3.1 70B on a single RTX 3090 using gpu-nvme-direct technique. Uses DMA (Direct Memory Access) to stream model weights directly from NVMe storage to GPU, completely bypassing CPU. Enables running 70B parameter models on consumer-grade GPUs with limited VRAM (24GB). Significantly lowers barrier for local large model deployment. Technical innovation optimizes traditional NVMe offloading by implementing direct NVMe-to-GPU DMA transfers.
author: xaskasdf

title: Claws are now a new layer on top of LLM agents
description: Andrej Karpathy describes Claws as a new layer above LLM agents. Quote: 'Just as LLM Agents represent a new stage in LLM development, Claws are undoubtedly another major leap forward for LLM Agents.' Claws are an AI execution system that can autonomously organize tools, complete workflows, and maintain long-term running states. Unlike LLM Agents that require manual guidance for single operations, Claws remove these limitations and enable true autonomous task execution. Karpathy bought a Mac mini specifically to experiment with Claws, sparking a buying spree in the developer community.
author: Andrej Karpathy

title: zclaw: Personal AI Assistant in Under 888 KB, Running on ESP32
description: MimiClaw project demonstrates running OpenClaw on ESP32-S3 microcontroller. Size: Under 888 KB, Cost: ~$5-10 USD, Power: ~0.5W. No Operating System required - runs pure C firmware with ESP-IDF and FreeRTOS. Implements full memory system with SOUL.md (personality), USER.md (user info), MEMORY.md (long-term memory), and daily notes. Features complete ReAct Agent Loop with Claude API for thinking, tool calling, and memory management. Capabilities include multi-turn conversations, hardware control (GPIO, I2C, SPI), temperature monitoring, relay control, web search, and OTA upgrades. Available on GitHub: memovai/mimiclaw.
author: tnm

title: a16z Partner Says 'Vibe Code' Theory is Wrong
description: Martin Casado, a16z general partner, challenges 'vibe coding' approach while expressing optimism about AI's impact on software engineering. Critics argue vibe coding creates 'trust debt' where senior engineers must reverse-engineer AI-generated code. Real-world failures include binary search implementations that failed under specific inputs, authentication systems that collapsed with complex features, and maintenance nightmares described as 'archaeology.' METR study found experienced developers using AI tools were 19% slower than those not using them. Core issue: problems only become apparent after system failures, creating undebbuggable, hard-to-extend, and painful-to-maintain production systems.
author: Martin Casado

title: Claude Code Founder Predicts Plan Mode May Become History
description: Claude Code founder Boris Cherny predicts that Plan Mode may become history as AI becomes smart enough to automatically decide when to plan vs. execute. The AI is reaching a point where it can autonomously determine the optimal approach without manual mode switching. This evolution reflects broader trend in AI coding tools: moving from explicit user control to intelligent, context-aware assistance. Highlights the rapid advancement of AI coding capabilities in early 2026.
author: Boris Cherny

title: 2026 GitHub Trending: AI Agents Explode, Traditional Languages Fade
description: GitHub 2026 trending analysis shows dramatic shift: TypeScript emerges as biggest winner, AI Agent projects see explosive growth. Top emerging tools: OpenClaw (potentially standard for personal AI), Superpowers/MCP (integration hub), Shannon and Pi-mono (core infrastructure). Declining trends: New programming languages (Zig, Mojo, Gleam) nearly disappeared from trending, traditional frontend frameworks (Next.js, Svelte, Astro) seeing significantly less interest, self-hosted tools (unless AI-related) experiencing sharp decline. 2026 called 'Year of Agent Engineering' - focus shifted from 'which model is strongest' to 'which Agent framework is most practical, engineered, and composable.' Skills-based architecture emerging: composable skill components similar to frontend UI component libraries.
author: GitHub Analysis

title: NanoClaw: 4000 Lines of Code for Safe, Efficient AI Execution Hub
description: Andrej Karpathy recommends NanoClaw, a lightweight AI execution system with core code of only 4000 lines. Designed as an AI execution hub that can autonomously organize tools, complete workflows, and maintain long-term running states. Part of broader OpenClaw ecosystem that includes various lightweight AI assistants (OpenClaw, PicoClaw, NanoClaw, MimiClaw). Represents trend toward minimal, efficient AI agent infrastructure that can run on resource-constrained hardware. Karpathy's endorsement sparked developer interest and experimentation with Mac mini deployments.
author: Andrej Karpathy

title: AI Coding Tools February 2026: Claude Opus 4.6 vs GPT-5.3-Codex
description: Major model releases in February 2026: Claude Opus 4.6 (Anthropic) with 400K context window, Agent Teams functionality for parallel task coordination, discovered 500+ high-risk vulnerabilities in open-source libraries, pricing $5/$25 per million tokens. GPT-5.3-Codex (OpenAI) with 25% speed improvement, new SWE-Bench Pro benchmark records, OpenAI Frontier platform for enterprise system integration. Gemini 3.1 Pro (Google) with 1M-2M+ context window, MMLU accuracy of 95.1% surpassing human expert average. Developer reports show 10x speed improvement for complex tasks. AI coding tools transitioning from experimental assistants to essential development partners.
author: Industry News

title: Vibe Coding Era Ends, Agentic Engineering Era Begins
description: Andrej Karpathy announces the end of Vibe Coding era and the beginning of Agentic Engineering. Evolution from intuition-based programming to more rigorous, precise AI-assisted programming methods. Timeline: November 2025: 80% manual coding + 20% AI; December 2025: 80% AI agents + 20% manual editing/refinement. Claude and Codex crossed consistency/coherence thresholds in late 2025, making AI-first coding suddenly viable. Represents fundamental shift in how developers approach AI-assisted programming - from casual 'vibes' to structured agent engineering.
author: Andrej Karpathy

title: LLMs Changing Software Development Constraints: Golden Age for Programming Language Research
description: Andrej Karpathy observes that for programming language and formal methods researchers, this is the most exciting time because LLMs are changing the constraints of software development. Insight: 'Agents know what an App is, Grandma doesn't need to.' Mentions nanochat project: 'Best $100 chat tool you can get' with 43,475 GitHub stars. Highlights how AI agents are abstracting away complexity, making software development more accessible while creating new opportunities for language and tool designers.
author: Andrej Karpathy

title: AI Coding Ecosystem February 2026: Market Dynamics and Enterprise Adoption
description: 2026 AI coding tools market analysis: Claude Code Opus 4.6 with Agent Teams (multi-instance parallel coordination) and 1M token context window, pricing $20-$200/month. Cursor 2.3+Composer 1.5 model introduces Shadow Workspace for background prediction and Plan Mode for design-first workflow, valued at $2.93B, $200M ARR. Copilot maintains 68% market share but limited context window. Windsurf supports 1M line codebase remote indexing. Trends: multi-agent coordination becoming standard, context windows dramatically expanding, Chinese enterprise alternatives (Baidu Qianfan, Moore Threads) emerging. Market shifting from 'AI assistant' to truly autonomous 'AI Agent' capable of planning, executing, and coordinating complex development tasks.
author: Tech Media

title: Claude Code Father vs Karpathy: Programming is Dead, Keyboards Gathering Dust
description: Conversation between Andrej Karpathy and Claude Code's Boris Cherny about how 20 years of programming workflow was disrupted in weeks: 80% of code now written by AI. 2026 predictions: dual evolution in model layer and Agent layer creating 'product effect' through multiplication rather than addition. Warning about 'Slopacolypse' risk: codebases may be flooded with AI-generated code that appears correct but has problems. Programmers evolving from 'code writers' to 'architects' and 'reviewers.' Highlights both opportunities and risks of AI-first development.
author: Andrej Karpathy, Boris Cherny

title: 2026 AI Agent Development Tools Ecosystem Panorama
description: Comprehensive analysis of 2026 AI Agent development tools ecosystem. Market evolution from 'AI assistant' to truly autonomous 'AI Agent' capable of planning, executing, and coordinating complex development tasks. Tool specialization trends: Claude Code CLI for minimal terminal scenarios, Cursor excels at project understanding and multi-file editing, OpenClaw focuses on long-term memory tasks. API aggregation platforms emerging to avoid multiple $20 subscriptions. Multi-agent coordination becoming standard. Chinese enterprise solutions (Baidu Qianfan Coding Plan, Moore Threads AI Coding Plan) providing localized alternatives. Ecosystem maturity improving: enterprise features (security, collaboration, billing) becoming standard.
author: Developer Community

title: Amjad Masad: From 'How' to 'What' Paradigm Shift in AI Coding
description: Replit CEO Amjad Masad articulates next stage of AI Agent evolution: shifting from 'telling AI how to do things' to 'telling AI what to do,' letting AI figure out execution path autonomously. Replit already has projects achieving zero human involvement from goal receipt to multi-agent collaboration to code deployment. Predicts AI Agent code generation, testing, and deployment speed growing 3.4x per quarter, approaching human developer limits. Expects human programming roles to potentially decline within ~2 years (early 2027). Future IDE will be task receiving, scheduling, and output interface system, not code editor.
author: Amjad Masad

title: Simon Willison's 6 AI Predictions: 1 Year Agents, 3 Years Pulitzer, 6 Years Crisis
description: Simon Willison's 6 major AI predictions: Within 1 year AI Agents will see practical application; within 3 years someone will win Pulitzer Prize using GenAI tools, journalists will use LLMs to process data; within 6 years AI will simplify art creation but if AGI replaces many human jobs it may trigger social crisis. His LLM command-line tool integrated into Kali Linux 2025.3 as 'llm-tools-nmap' experimental plugin. Highlights both opportunities and risks of AI advancement.
author: Simon Willison

title: Programming Dead? 2026 AI Technology Trends Predictions
description: Django co-founder Simon Willison's 2026 AI predictions: Within 1 year (late 2026), LLM-generated code quality will be 'undeniably excellent,' view that AI-generated code is garbage will become untenable, human manual coding proportion drops to single-digit percentages. Within 3 years: AI will be able to collaboratively develop browsers. Within 6 years: hand-written code may become history, programmers shift to architecture design and AI collaboration. Core themes: security issues (sandbox solutions), Jevons paradox, programmer role transformation. Represents optimistic but measured view of AI's impact on software development.
author: Simon Willison

title: AgentCgroup Research: First Systematic Analysis of AI Agent Resource Management
description: February 2026 arXiv paper presents first systematic study of AI agent resource dynamics. Key findings: tool calling accounts for 56-74% of latency, memory is primary bottleneck (not CPU), peak-to-average memory fluctuation reaches 15.4x. Reveals AI agent resource consumption patterns are fundamentally different from traditional applications, requiring new resource management and scheduling strategies. Important guidance for multi-agent system deployment in production environments. Addresses critical infrastructure challenges as AI agents move from experimental to production use.
author: Research Community

title: OpenAI vs Anthropic Same-Day Showdown: GPT-5.3-Codex vs Claude Opus 4.6
description: February 6, OpenAI and Anthropic released major coding models 20 minutes apart. OpenAI launched GPT-5.3-Codex with 25% speed improvement, integrated into Cursor/GitHub/VS Code, released macOS app, using Codex to build Codex achieving recursive AI development. Anthropic released Claude Opus 4.6 with Agent Teams functionality for task segmentation and parallel coordination, discovered 500+ high-risk security vulnerabilities, pricing $5/$25 per million tokens. Benchmarks: GPT-5.3-Codex sets records on SWE-Bench Pro, Claude Opus 4.6 leads on MRCR v2 and GDPval-AA. Developer reports show 10x speed improvement for complex tasks. Traditional security stocks fell (CrowdStrike down 6%). AI coding tools transitioning from experimental assistants to essential development partners.
author: Industry News

title: Lex Fridman Podcast #490: 2026 AI State of the Art Comprehensive Report
description: 4-hour super-long interview with guests Sebastian Raschka and Nathan Lambert. Covers: China-US AI competition, ChatGPT vs Claude vs Gemini vs Grok comparison, AI coding tools (Claude Code and Cursor), open-source vs closed-source LLMs, Transformer architecture evolution, Scaling Laws validity, AI training pipeline (pre-training/mid-training/post-training), post-training new techniques, career advice, AI industry 72+ hour work culture, text diffusion models, tool use and agents, continual learning, long context, robotics, AGI timeline (predicting human-level by 2030), future of programming jobs, business models, future of OpenAI/Anthropic/Google DeepMind/xAI/Meta, GPU computing and NVIDIA. Comprehensive overview of AI landscape in early 2026.
author: Lex Fridman, Sebastian Raschka, Nathan Lambert
